title: Phase 5b: LVS Checker (Layout vs Schematic)
description: Add Layout vs Schematic (LVS) verification to agentic-drc. Users upload a GDSII layout and a SPICE netlist, and the system verifies that the layout implements the same circuit. Uses KLayout's built-in LVS engine via the same subprocess pattern as DRC. Parses the .lvsdb report to extract device mismatches, net mismatches, and pin mismatches. Includes a frontend mismatch viewer.

existing_reuse: DRCRunner pattern (backend/core/drc_runner.py) — same subprocess + throttling approach

success_criteria: User can upload a SPICE netlist and run LVS against an existing DRC job's layout
LVS report is parsed into structured device-level and net-level mismatches
SKY130 LVS deck correctly extracts NMOS, PMOS, resistors, and capacitors from layout
Frontend displays mismatch list with device and net comparison
All existing 303+ tests still pass, no regressions
not_in_scope: LVS for PDKs other than SKY130 (future: GF180, ASAP7)
Cross-reference between DRC violations and LVS mismatches

userStories.title: LVS Runner (Subprocess)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an LVSRunner class that executes KLayout LVS in batch mode as a subprocess, mirroring DRCRunner's architecture. KLayout LVS command: `klayout -b -r deck.lvs -rd input=layout.gds -rd schematic=circuit.spice -rd report=lvs.lvsdb`. The runner handles: binary detection, command building, CPU throttling (same triple-layer as DRC), timeout enforcement, and result collection.
acceptanceCriteria: LVSRunner class in backend/core/lvs_runner.py with same __init__(klayout_binary, timeout) pattern as DRCRunner
run(gds_path, netlist_path, pdk, output_dir) method executes KLayout LVS subprocess
Command built as: klayout -b -r <deck.lvs> -rd input=<gds> -rd schematic=<spice> -rd report=<lvsdb>
CPU throttling applied: taskpolicy -b (macOS) + nice + cpulimit, same as DRCRunner
Async execution via asyncio.create_subprocess_exec (consistent with Phase 5a async DRC)
Timeout enforced (default 2700s), process killed on timeout, raises LVSError
LVSResult dataclass returned: report_path, returncode, stdout, stderr, duration_seconds, match (bool)
LVSError exception class with returncode and stderr fields
Test: mock subprocess → verify command built correctly with all -rd parameters
Test: timeout → verify process killed and LVSError raised
userStories.notes: Mirror backend/core/drc_runner.py closely. The LVS deck file lives in backend/pdk/configs/sky130/ alongside the DRC deck. LVS report format is .lvsdb (XML, different schema from .lyrdb DRC reports). The get_lvs_deck_path() method resolves the deck file from PDKConfig — add klayout_lvs_deck field to PDKConfig schema.

userStories.title: LVS Report Parser (.lvsdb XML)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to parse KLayout's .lvsdb report file into structured Python objects representing device mismatches, net mismatches, and pin mismatches. The .lvsdb format is XML with a different schema from .lyrdb (DRC reports). Need to extract: matched/unmatched devices, matched/unmatched nets, pin assignment errors, and overall match/nomatch status.
acceptanceCriteria: LVSReportParser class in backend/core/lvs_parser.py
parse_file(path) method returns LVSReport dataclass
LVSReport contains: match (bool), devices_matched (int), devices_mismatched (int), nets_matched (int), nets_mismatched (int), mismatches (list[LVSMismatch])
LVSMismatch dataclass with: type (missing_device|extra_device|net_mismatch|pin_mismatch|parameter_mismatch), name, expected (str), actual (str), details (str)
Device mismatches include: device type (nmos/pmos/res/cap), expected parameters (W, L), actual extracted parameters
Net mismatches include: layout net name, schematic net name, connection difference
Parser handles empty report (no mismatches = match)
Parser handles malformed XML gracefully (raises LVSParseError with context)
Test with sample .lvsdb XML fixture: verify all mismatches extracted correctly
Test with clean .lvsdb: verify match=true, mismatches=[]
userStories.notes: Study KLayout's .lvsdb XML format — it uses nested <item> elements with category attributes. Similar parsing approach to ViolationParser (backend/core/violation_parser.py) but different XML schema. Create test fixtures in tests/fixtures/ with sample .lvsdb files (one clean, one with mismatches). Models go in backend/core/lvs_models.py.

userStories.title: SKY130 LVS Deck
userStories.description: As a user running LVS on SKY130 layouts, I need a KLayout LVS deck that defines device extraction rules: which GDS layers form NMOS/PMOS transistors, poly resistors, MIM capacitors, and how nets are connected through vias and metal layers. This is the PDK-specific knowledge that tells KLayout how to interpret the layout as a circuit.
acceptanceCriteria: LVS deck file at backend/pdk/configs/sky130/sky130A.lvs
Extracts NMOS devices from: nwell, diffusion, poly layers with correct SKY130 layer numbers
Extracts PMOS devices from: pwell (or substrate), diffusion, poly layers
Extracts poly resistors from: poly + resistor ID layers
Defines connectivity: metal1 through metal5, via1-via4, contacts, local interconnect
Compares extracted netlist against input SPICE schematic
Outputs .lvsdb report file at the path specified by -rd report= parameter
PDKConfig for sky130 updated with klayout_lvs_deck field pointing to this file
pdk.json schema updated to include optional klayout_lvs_deck field
Test: run LVS with the deck on a known-good SKY130 inverter layout+schematic → match=true
Test: run LVS on layout with missing device → match=false, reports missing_device mismatch
userStories.notes: This is the hardest story — requires deep SKY130 PDK knowledge. Reference: the SKY130 DRC deck at backend/pdk/configs/sky130/sky130A_mr.drc for layer number conventions. KLayout LVS deck syntax uses: extract_devices(), connect(), compare(). The SKY130 open-source PDK may have existing LVS decks we can reference/vendor. Need test fixtures: a simple inverter (1 NMOS + 1 PMOS) as both GDS and SPICE.

userStories.title: LVS API Endpoints
userStories.description: As a user, I want API endpoints to upload a SPICE netlist, run LVS against a job's layout, and retrieve mismatch results. The flow: user has an existing DRC job with a GDS layout → uploads netlist → runs LVS → gets structured mismatch report.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/jobs/{job_id}/lvs/upload accepts SPICE netlist file upload, stores alongside GDS
POST /api/jobs/{job_id}/lvs/run triggers LVS (async, returns immediately like DRC)
Job status includes LVS state: lvs_running → lvs_complete or lvs_failed
GET /api/jobs/{job_id}/lvs/results returns parsed LVS report: match, device/net counts, mismatches list
Each mismatch in response includes: type, name, expected, actual, details
LVS requires DRC to have been run first (need the GDS path from job) — return 400 if no GDS
Netlist path stored in jobs table (add netlist_path column)
Error handling: missing netlist → 400, LVS timeout → 500 with explanation, no KLayout → 500
Test: upload netlist → run LVS → poll status → get results → verify mismatch structure
userStories.notes: New route file: backend/api/routes/lvs.py. Register in main.py. Add netlist_path column to jobs schema in database.py. LVS run uses async LVSRunner (from P5-LV-001). Results stored in a separate lvs_reports table or as a JSON file alongside the .lvsdb. Follow the same pattern as drc.py routes.

userStories.title: Frontend LVS Mismatch Viewer
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see LVS results in the frontend with a clear match/nomatch status and a mismatch list showing device-level and net-level comparison. The viewer should make it easy to understand what's different between the layout and the schematic.
acceptanceCriteria: LVS section appears in job detail page when LVS has been run
Match/nomatch banner with green check or red X
Summary stats: devices matched/mismatched, nets matched/mismatched
Mismatch table with columns: Type, Name, Expected, Actual, Details
Mismatch types are color-coded or icon-coded: missing_device (red), extra_device (orange), net_mismatch (yellow), pin_mismatch (blue), parameter_mismatch (grey)
Netlist upload form integrated into job detail page (file input for .spice/.sp files)
Run LVS button (disabled until netlist uploaded, shows spinner while running)
Frontend builds clean: npm run build succeeds with no TypeScript errors
Responsive layout — mismatch table scrollable on small screens
userStories.notes: Existing frontend is React + Vite at /frontend. Follow existing component patterns for the DRC violation viewer. LVS API calls: POST upload, POST run, GET results. Consider a collapsible detail row for each mismatch showing the full expected vs actual comparison.
